author: @stevecummins324
text:
What rpm are these likely to get upto? Rotative machines usually more compact if turned faster... 

Something akin to old tech of a Bike dynamo.  Could maybe be made with a few powerful magnets mounted alternative N/S on  faces of a small driving disk, that will be spun. If magnets are little cylinders... They'll fit nicely into  holes drilled on a pitch circle. Rubber bands stretched around circumference would ensure it  turns if sprung against inner/outer of a spinning "drum".. Smaller magnet disk equals considerably increased  rpm. 

Coils fixed in line with pitch circle on   magnets. Maybe use C shape former over edge of disk to help return/ concentrate flux through the coils. Old transformer lamination prob best, but anything "iron" should help. Including metal wire coat hangers.
If using flux concentrator leave an arc/segment, free of coils so driving disk is not completely enclosed, and  can be pressed against large drum/wheel.  Output waveform then not simple sinusoidal... But if rectified to DC... not a problem.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @MarkSeve
text: Always enjoy watching your videos Robert.  Hope the finger will stay attacked, Cyano Actrilite is good for sealing wounds. Looking good, can't wait to see the output of this project.  Take care, and be safe.

replies.author: @MarkSeve

LOL   I also figured something like this was going to happen, your pace had been approaching frantic for the last couple videos.   Everything ok?  And don't worry, I kind of read people, I'm sure no one else noticed.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
This looks really cool, the best part is that it is made for something designed to be put on the roof so no permits would be required in countries with laws about anything above the roofline. This should also have the potential to put out a good amount of power also.
I have a feeling that you may need to arrange the magnets in an alternating N, S, N, S etc.. position.
Great video Rob, eagerly looking forward to seeing this completed.

replies.author: @unicornadrian1358
replies.text: It might also need some extra weight to keep momentum when current is being drawn.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep - probably will use just good old NSN arrangement

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I'll use it to charge a battery or capacitor and draw the load from that

replies.author: @unicornadrian1358
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith to keep the current draw low?

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I would like to see it done in a couple of different formats to gauge the best usage. One way would be to wire every second coil together to have two lines that could then be rectified individually before wiring in series. The N, S, N, S orientation of the magnets is definitely the best way to go. I am ordering some magnets tomorrow to try something similar but using a standard windmill type design with staggered 30cm carbon fibre propellers designed for drones. Thinking of using 4 x twin blade propellers at 45 degree offsets.
